title: The Pancavimsa Brahmana (Complete)
translator_full: Dr. W. Caland, Emeritus Professor of Sanskrit in the University of Utrecht
description: The Pancavimsa Brahmana (Brahmana of Twenty-Five Chapters) is an important prose theological work belonging to the Samaveda tradition. It contains ritual instructions, philosophical discussions, mythological narratives, and Vedic explanations written in Vedic prose (not poetry). This text is crucial for understanding Vedic ritual practices and brahmanical philosophy.
language: English translation with Sanskrit text analysis
significance: Unlike the Rigveda and Yajurveda which are poetic/mantra collections, the Brahmanas (including Pancavimsa) provide the prose explanations and philosophical background. Essential for understanding how Sanskrit handles narrative descriptions, explanations, abstract philosophical concepts, practical ritual instructions, and theological arguments.

structure.introduction: Comprehensive chapters on Samaveda texts, traditions, and schools
structure.main_content: 25 main chapters covering various Samaveda-related topics

usage_for_rag: Provides Vedic prose examples for understanding ritual language, philosophical ideas, proper nouns (gods, sages, ritual names), conversational vocabulary beyond poetic meters, and brahmanical theological concepts
notes: This translation by W. Caland (1931) is the standard English rendering of the Pancavimsa Brahmana. The text contains valuable Sanskrit terminology and proper nouns relevant to Vedic studies. All 25 chapters are included in the complete work.
